resources.string: GipfelStürmer
100 Games for Little Climbers
Search game, category or keyword
All Games
Indoor
Outdoor
Pick a Random Game
%d games found
Easy
Medium
Hard
Back
Favorite
Rules
Description
Indoor
Outdoor
Reset
Start
Pause
All
Young
Middle
Older
Ages 4–6
Ages 7–10
Ages 11+
Age Group
All
Climbing
Warm-Up
Strength
Game Type
The Blind Passenger
A climber climbs blind (or blindfolded) while a partner gives directions.

One climber gets blindfolded. The partner stands below and gives instructions like "right hand up", "left foot to the left". The goal is to complete the route without seeing. Switch roles after each round.
Hold Color Roulette
Only holds of a specific color may be used. First one to the top wins.
Choose a hold color. All climbers may only use holds of that color. Footholds are free to choose. Whoever completes the route first or gets the furthest wins the round.
Climbing Memory
The first climber makes a move, the next repeats it and adds one more.

The first climber makes a move (e.g. right hand on a specific hold). The next climber repeats this move and adds their own. This continues until someone makes a mistake. Whoever remembers the sequence the longest wins.
Spider
Traverse across the wall without touching the ground. Whoever gets the furthest wins.

Everyone starts at the same point and climbs sideways (traverses) across the wall. The ground must not be touched. Whoever falls off is out. Whoever gets the furthest without touching the ground wins.
Squirrel
Collect small objects on the wall while climbing. Whoever collects the most wins.
Attach small objects (clothespins, stickers) to various holds on the wall. Climbers must collect as many as possible and store them in a pocket without falling off. Whoever collects the most wins.
Climbing Twister
Like Twister, but on the climbing wall! Colors and limbs are called out.

A game leader calls combinations like "Right hand on red" or "Left foot on blue". Climbers must follow the instruction without falling off. Whoever falls or can\'t execute the instruction is eliminated.
Climbing Cop
One climber is the "cop" and tries to catch the others on the wall.

One child is the cop and climbs after the others. Whoever is touched by the cop is caught and must come down. The last one on the wall wins and becomes the new cop. Only play in the traverse area (low height)!
Lava Floor
The floor is lava! Climb from one point to another without touching the ground.
Mark start and finish points on the wall. The floor is "lava" and must not be touched. Climbers must make the entire way along the wall. Whoever touches the floor must start over.
One-Armed Bandit
Climb an easy route with only one arm. Left or right is determined beforehand.
Choose an easy route. Determine which arm may be used before starting. The other arm stays behind the back. Both feet may be used freely. Whoever completes the route tries the other arm.
Climbing Karaoke
Climb a route while singing a song. Whoever stops singing or falls off is out.
Each climber chooses a song and must sing it while climbing. Whoever stops singing or falls off is eliminated. Others judge climbing performance and singing. The best overall impression wins!
Freeze!
Music plays – when it stops, everyone on the wall must freeze. Whoever moves is out.

Play music while the children climb on the wall. When the music stops, everyone must freeze immediately. Whoever moves or falls off is out. Music on again – keep climbing. Last climber on the wall wins.
Climbing Relay
Two teams race in a relay climb. High-fiving on the ground counts as handover.
Form two equal-sized teams. One climber per team climbs a route up and back down, then high-fives the next. The team where all members finish first wins. If you fall, restart from the bottom.
Rock Tic-Tac-Toe
Play Tic-Tac-Toe on the climbing wall with colored markers.
Draw a 3x3 grid with tape on the wall (in the traverse area). Two players climb alternately and place their colored markers (clothespins etc.) in a field. Whoever gets three in a row wins!
Climbing Dice
Roll the dice and climb as many holds further as the dice shows.

Each climber rolls the dice on the ground and then climbs exactly as many holds further as the dice shows. Then roll again. Whoever reaches a certain route or height first wins. On a 6, you get a bonus move.
Shadow Climber
Climb exactly the same moves as the person before you. Only mimicking is allowed!
The first climber slowly climbs a route. The second climber starts shortly after and must use exactly the same holds and footholds. If a different hold is used or a move is forgotten, you\'re out.
Mountaineer Quiz
Answer quiz questions while hanging on the wall. Wrong answer = come down!

A game leader asks questions while the children hang on the wall. Whoever answers correctly may continue climbing. Whoever answers incorrectly must go one hold lower. Whoever reaches the top first or hangs the longest wins.
Climbing Bingo
Everyone gets a bingo card with climbing tasks. First to complete a row calls Bingo!
Create bingo cards with tasks like "Climb with only 3 holds", "Touch the highest hold", "Traverse 2 meters". Children climb and check off completed tasks. Whoever fills a row first calls BINGO!
Time Race
Who climbs the route fastest? The stopwatch decides!
Choose a route everyone knows. Each climber is timed individually with the stopwatch. The fastest time wins. Tip: Use the app\'s built-in timer! Fair play: Everyone starts from the same point.
Nature Explorer
Climb outdoors on real rock and discover animals, plants and special stones.

When climbing on real rock: Create a list of things to find beforehand (specific stone, moss, beetle, etc.). During climbing, discoveries are collected. Whoever finds the most items from the list wins.
Climbing Robot
Your partner is the robot – they only climb when you give the right command.

One child is the "programmer", the other the "robot". The robot may only move according to commands: "Right hand up 30cm", "Left foot right 20cm". The robot executes exactly what is said. Goal: Complete the route only through commands!
Climbing Alphabet
Find holds that look like letters and spell your name.
Look at the holds on the wall. Find holds that look like letters. Everyone tries to spell their name by touching the corresponding holds. Whoever spells their name completely first wins.
Hold Detective
A hold on the wall is secretly marked. Find it while climbing!

A game leader secretly marks a hold (e.g. with a small sticker on the underside). Climbers must find the marked hold by examining holds from all sides while climbing. Whoever finds the sticker first wins.
Climbing Yoga
Hold various yoga poses on the wall. Whoever holds out the longest wins.

Children climb to a comfortable position on the wall. The game leader calls poses: "Let go of one leg", "Hold with only one hand", "Back to the wall". Each pose must be held for 10 seconds. Whoever falls off is eliminated.
Summit Messenger
Deliver a message by climbing from one point to another – without losing it!
Each child gets a small note (the "message") that must be transported in their mouth or pocket. Climb from start to finish without losing the message. Whoever loses the message must start over.
Climbing Domino
The next climber must start at the hold where the previous one stopped.

The first climber climbs freely and stops at any hold. The next climber must start at exactly that hold and continue climbing from there until they stop. This continues. Whoever can\'t connect is eliminated.
Silent Climber
Climb as quietly as possible. Every noise when grabbing or stepping gives a point deduction.

Children climb a route and try to be as quiet as possible. An observer counts every audible noise (clicking, scratching, groaning). Whoever makes the fewest noises wins. Promotes clean climbing technique!
Climbing Simon Says
"Simon says: Right hand on yellow!" Only when Simon says it may you move.

Like "Simon Says", but on the wall. The game leader gives instructions. Only with "Simon says..." may the instruction be followed. Whoever moves without "Simon says" must come down. Last climber on the wall wins.
Climbing Obstacle Course
Build a course with various stations: climbing, balancing, jumping.
Build a course with stations: 1. Traverse on the wall, 2. Balance on a bench, 3. Climb through a hoop, 4. Route up and down. Each child is timed with the stopwatch. The fastest overall time wins.
Hold Counter
Complete the route with as few holds as possible. Every hold is counted.
Choose a route. Each climber tries to complete it with as few holds as possible. An observer counts every hold used. Whoever needs the fewest holds wins. Promotes efficiency and planning!
Summit Stormer Challenge
The ultimate challenge: Complete as many routes as possible in a set time.

Set the timer to 10 minutes. Each climber tries to climb as many different routes as possible in that time. Each completed route earns a point. Difficult routes earn bonus points. Whoever has the most points is the Summit Stormer!
Frog Jump
Jump like a frog from hold to hold on the wall. Who gets the highest?
Start on low holds. Each child jumps with both feet from hold to hold upward. Whoever misses a hold or falls off starts over. Whoever gets the highest wins. Only play at a safe jumping height!
Crawling Beetle
Climb on all fours – hands and feet flat – like a beetle along the wall.
Imagine you are beetles! Climb with flat hands and feet (don\'t grip holds, just press flat) as far as possible along the wall. Whoever gets the furthest without falling off wins.
Monkey Swing
Traverse the wall using only your hands – feet stay in the air. How far can you go?
Start at the left edge of the traverse wall. Only hands may touch holds – feet hang free. Swing from hold to hold. Whoever gets the furthest without touching the ground is the best monkey!
Shooting Star
Touch 5 different colored holds as fast as possible. The clock is ticking!
Choose 5 holds in different colors and mark them with tape. Each child starts on the ground, climbs and touches all 5 holds in any order. Whoever touches all 5 fastest has made a shooting star!
Rainbow Climber
Climb holds in rainbow color order: Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, Blue!
Each child climbs the wall touching holds in order: Red – Orange – Yellow – Green – Blue – Purple. If a color is skipped, start over. Whoever completes the full rainbow wins!
Soap Bubble Route
Climb as softly and quietly as if you were keeping soap bubbles from popping.
An observer blows real soap bubbles next to the climbing wall. Children climb an easy route trying to keep bubbles alive through calm, gentle movements. Whoever keeps the most bubbles alive wins!
Animal Paws
Imitate animal movements while climbing. Bear, cat, spider – what are you?
The leader calls an animal. Children climb like that animal: Bear (slow, heavy), Cat (sneaky, elegant), Spider (sideways, fast), Frog (jumping). The leader judges who best portrays the animal.
Color Worm
Always alternate: Red – Blue – Yellow. Break the sequence and you\'re out!
Each child climbs and must use holds in the sequence Red – Blue – Yellow – Red – Blue – Yellow. An observer checks. Whoever grabs a wrong color must start over. First to reach the top wins.
Dancing Mountains
Dance to music and when it stops, pose as a mountain peak or a climber!
Music plays, everyone dances freely. When the music stops, the leader calls "Mountain" (everyone reaches arms up sharply) or "Climber" (everyone mimics climbing grips). Whoever is too slow is out.
Jumping Jack Race
Who does the most jumping jacks in 30 seconds? Go!

Everyone starts at the same time. Each person does as many jumping jacks as possible in 30 seconds. A partner counts. After time, compare results. Whoever has the most wins the round. Play multiple rounds!
Penguin Walk
Walk with arms pressed tightly against your body to the goal and back – like a penguin!

Everyone presses arms tight against their body (elbows at side, hands forward). Walk a course: slalom through cones, over a line, back to start. Fastest wins. Arms coming loose = penalty (5 jumping jacks).
Hop Fun
Hop on one leg through different fields. Fall and switch legs!

Draw or place fields on the ground (tape or hoops). Everyone hops on one leg through the fields. Whoever lands on both feet or hops out must continue on the other leg. First to complete the course wins.
Rocket Launch
Lie flat on the ground, count down from 5 and shoot up like a rocket!

Everyone lies flat on the ground. Together count backward: 5 – 4 – 3 – 2 – 1 – Ignition! At "Ignition" everyone jumps up simultaneously and stretches arms up. Then immediately do 10 squats. Whoever stands up fastest wins!
Spider Walk
Walk on hands and feet, belly facing up. Can you be as fast as a spider?
Everyone gets into "crab position": hands and feet on the ground, belly facing up. Navigate a course in this position. Fastest wins. Variation: Balance a small ball on your belly while doing it!
Magic Mirror
Two children face each other and mirror each other\'s movements – perfectly synchronized!

Two children face each other. Child A makes slow movements (raise arms, squats, turns). Child B tries to mirror exactly the same movements. After 1 minute, switch. The others judge who was most synchronized.
Little Lion
Knee push-ups – as many as possible! Roar like a lion with every rep!

Everyone does knee push-ups and roars softly with each rep. Whoever completes 5 can mime a lion mane. Whoever does 10 is a big lion. Whoever does more than 15 is the king of climbing beasts! Animal sounds for motivation allowed!
Bridge Builder
Do a bridge pose and hold it as long as possible. Who builds the most stable bridge?

Everyone gets into bridge position (back and hips raised, hands and feet on the ground). Whoever holds the longest wins. Variation: The leader places a light object on the bridge – whoever drops it is out.
Wall Sit
Sit without a chair with your back against the wall and hold on. Who holds the longest?

Everyone leans back against the wall, knees at 90 degrees, as if sitting on an invisible chair. Time is measured. Whoever holds the position longest wins. Hands may not touch the wall. Giving up = elimination.
Log Roll
Lie on the mat, stretch your body and roll like a log. Who stays straight?

Everyone lies on the ground, arms stretched above head, body like a plank. Now roll sideways – without bending arms or legs! Whoever stays straightest and rolls the furthest wins. Multiple rounds for time.
Frog Power Jump
From a deep squat, jump as far as possible and land softly like a frog!

Everyone stands at a line. From deep squat (fingertips touch the ground) jump as far forward as possible. Mark the landing spot. Whoever jumps the furthest wins. 3 attempts, the furthest counts. Soft landing = bonus!
Moonboard Starter
Climb a set boulder route with coordinate instructions from your partner.
Partner calls coordinates (e.g. "Row 3, Hold 2"). The climber must find and use exactly that hold. Then the partner calls the next. 5 moves total. Whoever hits all 5 correct holds wins. Then switch!
Campus Traverse
Traverse the wall using only your hands on slopers – feet stay in the air!

Find a traverse with small ledges. Both hands on ledges, feet hanging free. Traverse as far as possible sideways. Whoever falls off is measured for distance. Feet may not touch the wall. Whoever gets the furthest wins.
Double Hold Challenge
Each hand must touch two holds simultaneously. Can you complete the route?

Find holds close enough to touch two with one hand. Climb a route where each hand must always touch two holds. Whoever completes the route has passed the challenge. Variation: Feet must also touch two footholds each!
Reverse Climbing
Climb a route from top to bottom – backwards! The top is your start.

Choose a route everyone knows. Start at the top (with belay) and climb the route in reverse order downward. Every hold must be used in the reversed sequence. Whoever completes the route completely in reverse has passed the challenge!
Blind Traverse
Traverse blindfolded – your partner gives instructions!

One climber is blindfolded and climbs in the traverse area (low height, max 1 meter). The partner stands alongside and gives precise instructions: "Right hand 20 cm to the right", "Left foot up". After 5 moves, switch. Safety first!
Non-Stop Ascent
Climb a route without pausing even a second. No hanging, no resting!

Choose a medium route. Each climber must complete the route from bottom to top without any pause. The moment you stop (even briefly), the attempt is invalid. Movement must flow constantly. Whoever completes the route fluidly wins!
Climbing Bus
Climb one behind the other – exactly one hold-length gap to the person in front!

3–4 children climb simultaneously one behind the other. Each child must keep exactly one hold gap to the next – not too close, not too far. Whoever loses the gap must stop and wait. First to the top becomes the new bus driver!
Spiderman Style
Climb as close to the wall as possible – no arched back, body flat like Spiderman!

An observer checks how close the climber stays to the wall. A stick is held horizontally behind the climber\'s back – whoever touches the stick has too much arch. Whoever completes a route without touching the stick climbs like Spiderman!
Safe Jump
Learn to jump from various heights in a controlled way and land softly.

Jump from increasing heights: first 50 cm, then 1 m, then 1.5 m. At landing: feet shoulder-width, slight bend in knees, hands forward. Whoever lands softest and shows the most pronounced knee bend gets the most points from the observer.
Rhythm Climbing
Climb in time to a metronome or clapping. Every beat = one move!

The leader claps or sets a metronome (60 bpm). At each beat, the climber must make one move – not faster, not slower. Whoever loses the rhythm (too fast or too slow) is out. Increase the tempo after each round!
Volcano Eruption
Everyone jumps up simultaneously and lands in volcano pose – arms explode outward!

Everyone stands in a circle. The leader counts: "3 – 2 – 1 – Eruption!" Everyone jumps simultaneously as high as possible and lands with arms spread wide (the exploding volcano). Whoever jumps highest and has the most dramatic pose wins!
Tag and Climb
Classic tag – but you\'re safe while climbing! Must touch at least 3 holds.

One child is "it" and tries to tag the others. Whoever is climbing on the wall (touching at least 3 holds) is temporarily safe and cannot be tagged. Once they come down, they can be tagged. Whoever is tagged becomes the new "it".
Number Sprint
The leader calls a number – everyone sprints to the field with that number!

Place numbered fields around the room (1–10). The leader calls a number, everyone sprints immediately to that field. Last to arrive does 5 jumping jacks. Then immediately the next number. Variation: Call two numbers in a row for more chaos!
Clap Circle
Clap and stomp in different rhythms in a circle – lose the beat and you\'re out!

Everyone stands in a circle. The leader sets a rhythm (e.g. 2× clap, 1× stomp). Everyone joins in. Whoever loses the rhythm or is too late is out or does an extra task. The rhythm gets more complex each round.
Energy Ball
Throw an invisible energy ball! The receiver must react immediately and keep playing.

Everyone stands in a circle. Person A forms an imaginary ball and "throws" it with a sound to Person B. B must react immediately, "catch" the ball and continue playing. Whoever reacts too slowly or drops the ball is out. Speed up!
Juggler Warm-Up
Throwing exercises with balls in pairs: easy first, then harder. Who drops none?

Pairs stand facing each other. Round 1: simple throw, Round 2: hop before catching, Round 3: spin before catching, Round 4: briefly close eyes when throwing. Whoever drops the ball starts from Round 1. Winner is the pair that gets the furthest.
Moon Stations
Various warm-up exercises at stations – as if you had moon gravity!

Set up 4 stations: 1. Slow-motion running (as if everything is slowed down), 2. Super long jump (as if on the moon), 3. Weightless jumping jacks, 4. Moon push-ups. 45 seconds per station. Catch breath, then repeat from the start!
Pull-Up Ladder
1 pull-up, then 2, then 3... how high can you climb the ladder?

Each climber first does 1 pull-up, then 2, then 3 etc. 30 seconds rest between sets. Whoever can\'t complete a set is out. The highest number of successful pull-ups in a set wins. Note the result – compare next session. Light assistance is allowed.
Dead Hang
Hang from a hold or bar – who holds the longest?

Everyone hangs simultaneously from a bar or hold. Time is measured. Whoever lets go first is out. Allowed: active hang (slightly bent arms) or passive hang (straight arms). Last one hanging wins. Record time for next round!
Plank Variations
Hold various plank positions: normal, side, one arm. Who holds the longest?

Round 1: normal plank (60 sec), Round 2: side plank left (30 sec), Round 3: side plank right (30 sec), Round 4: plank with alternating leg raises. Whoever completes all rounds wins. Hips may not sag, body stays straight!
Push-Up Variations
Classic, diamond, wide – three push-up variations of 5 reps each. Can you do them all?

Round 1: 5 classic push-ups, Round 2: 5 diamond push-ups (hands form diamond), Round 3: 5 wide push-ups (hands wide apart). 20 sec rest between rounds. Whoever completes all 15 reps with correct technique wins!
Dead Bug
On your back, slowly lower arms and legs – keep your core tight!

Everyone lies on their back, arms straight up, legs at 90 degrees. Slowly lower one arm and the opposite leg toward the ground without touching it. Back stays flat! Whoever does 10 clean reps is the Dead Bug Champion!
Wall Walk
From push-up position, walk your feet up the wall – as high as possible!

Start in push-up position with feet against the wall. Walk feet up the wall while walking hands toward the wall simultaneously. How close can you get to vertical? Whoever gets closest to the vertical position wins. Mind your safety!
Onsight Challenge
Climb an unknown route without reading it first. Can you do it first try?

The leader prepares a route no one knows. No reading, no watching! Each climber attempts the route cold – on the first try. Whoever succeeds has done it "onsight". Points: Onsight = 3, second attempt = 2, third = 1. Most points wins!
Flash Attempt
Watch someone climb once – then do the route on your first try!

Person A climbs a route (may fail). Person B watches carefully and may ask questions. Then Person B attempts the route – on their first try. If successful, it\'s a "flash". Whoever gets the most flashes in the group wins!
Limit Boulder
Find your personal limit boulder and document it. Can you do it next week?

Everyone finds a boulder they just barely can\'t complete. Document this route (photo, description). In the next session, try again. Whoever completes their limit boulder sets a new one. Make progress visible!
Coordinate Route
Climb a route described only by hold coordinates. No pointing allowed!

The leader marks holds with coordinates (column A–F, row 1–8). A route is described as a coordinate list: "A3 – C2 – E4 – B6". The climber must find and climb the route using the coordinates. Whoever climbs the route correctly wins!
Power Traverse
Traverse as far as possible without pause – pure power endurance!

Start at the left end of the traverse wall. Climb as far right as possible without pause or slipping. Whoever gets the furthest wins. Measure distance in holds. Next week everyone tries to beat their personal record.
System Board Duel
Both climb the same route – who makes more moves before falling?

Two climbers climb simultaneously (side by side) on the same route. They start at the same time and moves are counted. Whoever falls first loses. On equal falls, whoever made more moves wins. 3 rounds, then points are added.
Dynamo Training
Train dynamic moves: from small controlled ones to big explosive dynos!

Progression: 1. Small dyno (one hold distance), 2. Medium dyno (two hold distances), 3. Large dyno (three hold distances), 4. Coordinated dyno (both hands switch simultaneously). 3 attempts per level. Whoever reaches level 4 has completed dynamo training!
Technical Analysis
Climb a route then analyze every single move. What was good, what was bad?

Person A climbs a medium route. Person B watches and takes notes: Which holds were used well? Where were the problems? Then both discuss every move. Person A climbs again with the improvement suggestions. Then switch!
Route Setting
Set your own route for fellow climbers and explain the idea behind it.

Each child may set their own route (mark with tape or chalk). The route must be fair and interesting for fellow climbers. The "route setter" then explains the key moves. Everyone climbs the route. Who rates others\' routes most creatively?
Lead Simulation
Simulate lead climbing movements: clipping the rope, looking up, controlled lowering.

With a climbing rope (no real lead!) climb a route and simulate clipping: pick up rope, hold to the side, "clip". At the top: release hands, let go of wall and be lowered in a controlled manner. Practice for future real lead climbing!
Athlete Circuit
5 stations for 45 seconds each: jump rope, burpees, climbing, push-ups, sprinting.

Station 1: Jump rope (45 sec), Station 2: Burpees (45 sec), Station 3: Traverse climbing (45 sec), Station 4: Push-ups (45 sec), Station 5: 20m sprint back and forth (45 sec). 15 sec rest between stations. 2 rounds. Whoever completes 2 rounds is an athlete!
Partner Stretch
Deep stretching in pairs – gently getting into the stretch with mutual assistance.

5 stretches in pairs: 1. Back-to-back sit, 2. Shoulder stretch with help, 3. Thigh stretch with support, 4. Side stretch, 5. Hip opener. Hold each stretch 30 seconds. Partner applies gentle pressure, never jerky. Communication is key!
Coordination Ladder
Different patterns through a coordination ladder on the ground – fast and precise!

Place a coordination ladder (or tape) on the ground. Different patterns: 1. One foot per field, 2. Both feet per field, 3. Sideways, 4. Crossover step. Each person does all 4 patterns. Fastest and most error-free wins the round. Timekeeper needed!
Balance Board
Stand on a balance board and hold various poses. How long can you balance?

On the balance board (or unstable surface): Round 1: just stand (30 sec), Round 2: lift one leg (15 sec each side), Round 3: close eyes (10 sec), Round 4: catch a ball (10 throws). Points for each successfully completed round!
Medicine Ball Athletics
Medicine ball throws, squats and rotations for strength and coordination.

With a light medicine ball (2–4 kg): 1. Chest passes in pairs (10×), 2. Overhead throws upward (10×), 3. Russian twists seated (20×), 4. Squat with medicine ball (15×), 5. Slam ball on mat (10×). 3 rounds. Choose weight based on ability!
Max Pull-Ups
How many pull-ups can you do in one set? Your personal record!

Each person has one attempt. Full pull-up: arms straight at bottom, chin above bar at top. No swinging, no kipping. Whoever does the most reps wins. Record result – compare next session. Honesty in execution is mandatory!
Fingerboard Training
Hang on various hold depths for 10 seconds each. Build grip strength!

Progression on the fingerboard: 1. Full crimp (all 4 fingers, 10 sec), 2. Two-thirds depth (10 sec), 3. Half depth (10 sec), 4. Tiny ledge (10 sec, if possible). 3 minutes rest between ledges. Never hang to the point of pain! Technique before intensity.
One-Arm Hang
Hang with one hand from the bar or hold – how long does each side hold?

Always with belay! On a thick hold or bar: hang with one hand as long as possible. Measure time. Then the other hand. Note the difference. Goal: develop both sides equally. Not to exhaustion – maximum 80% of maximum hang time.
Push and Pull
Superset: push-ups (push) + pull-ups (pull) – the perfect duo for climbers!

Superset without rest: 5 push-ups directly followed by 5 pull-ups. That\'s 1 round. 3 minutes rest. Then Round 2. 3 rounds total. Whoever completes all 3 rounds with correct technique is on the right track. Start with fewer reps if needed!
Core Power
L-sit, front lever attempt and hollow body hold – core strength for elite climbers!

1. L-sit (legs straight, lift off ground, 10 sec), 2. Hollow body hold (on back, body like a banana, 30 sec), 3. Front lever attempt (hang from bar, legs to horizontal, as far as possible). 3 attempts each. Document progress weekly!
Explosive Power
Train clap push-ups and explosive pull-ups – speed strength for dynamic moves!

Clap push-ups: 3 sets of 5 reps. Explosive pull-ups (as fast as possible upward, controlled downward): 3 sets of 3 reps. 2 minutes rest between sets. Only if basic strength is already present! Technique first, explosiveness comes naturally.
Shoulder Protocol
Shoulder health: rotation, elevation, depression and stabilization for injury-free climbing.

Daily protocol (10 min): 1. Shoulder rotation with band (15× each side), 2. Shoulder blade elevation and depression (15×), 3. Y-T-W-L exercises on the ground (10× each), 4. Theraband external rotation (15× each side). Light resistance, high movement quality!
Grip Strength Training
Train all grip types: closed crimp, open hand, sidepull, pinch.

4 stations: 1. Crimp (closed grip, 10 sec, 3×), 2. Open hand (10 sec, 3×), 3. Sidepull (10 sec each side, 3×), 4. Pinch (thumb + fingers, 10 sec, 3×). 2 min rest per station. Never hang to pain! Stretch after training.
Antagonist Training
Train finger extensors and forearm opposing muscles – prevention against overuse!

3 exercises: 1. Finger extensors with band (open fingers against resistance, 20×, 3 sets), 2. Wrist extension (light weight, 15×, 3 sets), 3. Forearm pronation/supination with hammer (15×, 3 sets). Do after every climbing training. Prevention beats recovery!
Max Strength Test
Standardized test: max pull-ups + max push-ups + max hang time.

Test in this order (5 min rest each): 1. Maximum pull-ups in one set, 2. Maximum push-ups in one set, 3. Maximum hang time (straight arms). Document all results. Repeat monthly to measure progress. Technique must be correct!
Strength Endurance
3 rounds: 10 pull-ups + 20 push-ups + 30 sit-ups each. No rest between exercises!

One round: 10 pull-ups, immediately 20 push-ups, immediately 30 sit-ups. No rest between exercises. 3 minutes rest between rounds. 3 rounds total. Whoever completes all 3 rounds with correct technique has exceptional climber strength endurance!
Camp4 Full Program
The complete training protocol of a professional climber – for an entire session!

Full session (90 min): 1. Warm-up 15 min (Juggler Warm-Up + Magic Mirror), 2. Fingerboard 20 min (Grip Strength Training), 3. Boulder training 30 min (Onsight + Limit + Technical Analysis), 4. Strength 15 min (Push and Pull + Core Power), 5. Cool-down 10 min (Partner Stretch + Shoulder Protocol).
